A.rule: Start at 4; successive differences are squares of consecutive primes 3,5,7,11,...
Interleave squares n^2 and factorials n!: 1^2,1!,2^2,2!,...

B.rule: Add successive prime squares, starting with 3²
Multiply by successive integers and alternate subtracting 1, adding 1
Odd terms are consecutive squares; even terms are consecutive factorials

C.rule: Start at 4; successive differences are squares of consecutive primes starting with 3^2

D.rule: Catalan numbers C_n = binom(2n,n)/(n+1), starting at C_0
Start at 4; successive differences are squares of consecutive odd primes 3,5,7,11,...
Odd positions are successive squares; even positions are successive factorials

E.rule: Start at 4; add successive squares of primes 3,5,7,11,...
Odd-position terms are consecutive squares; even-position terms are consecutive factorials

failures.A: Correct Catalan continuation; no arithmetic mismatch.
failures.B: Correct continuation, but rule description is less specific.
failures.C: Correct continuation and rule, slightly less explicit than D.
failures.D: Exact Catalan definition and prefix coverage.
failures.E: Correct Catalan continuation, but indexing description is less explicit.

exactness.reason: All proposals are consistent; D gives the most exact definition.

failures.B: Correct arithmetic, but the prime sequence is less explicitly qualified.
failures.C: Correct rule and continuation; slightly less precise than A/D.
failures.D: Correct consecutive odd-prime squares and continuation.
failures.E: Correct rule and continuation; slightly less explicit than D.

exactness.reason: All proposals match; D most precisely specifies the intended prime sequence.

failures.A: Mismatch at the first recurrence step: gives 19 instead of 15.
failures.B: Correct continuation, but the rule is less formally specified.
failures.C: Correct multipliers and alternating signs; less general than D.
failures.D: Exact recurrence matches every prefix term and continuation.
failures.E: Mismatch immediately: its recurrence gives a2=2 instead of 4.

exactness.reason: D states the exact recurrence; A and E fail at the earliest recurrence check.

failures.B: Correct rule and continuation, with slightly less positional precision.
failures.C: Correct rule and continuation, but less explicit than D.
failures.D: Exact odd/even positional rule matches the prefix and continuation.
failures.E: Correct rule and continuation; equivalent but slightly less concise.

exactness.reason: All proposals are arithmetically consistent; D gives the clearest exact positional rule.

failures.A: Correct Catalan identification but omits indexing detail.
failures.B: Correct but less explicit about the starting term.
failures.C: Correct but redundant wording without added precision.
failures.D: No substantive failure; exact formula and indexing are given.
failures.E: Correct compact identification, slightly less explicit than D.

simplicity.reason: All proposals identify Catalan numbers; D gives the exact conventional formula and starting index.

failures.A: Correct, but more verbose than necessary.
failures.B: Correct compact rule, though prime sequence is slightly less explicit.
failures.D: Correct, with unnecessary restriction to odd primes.
failures.E: No substantive failure; concise and accurate.

simplicity.reason: E states the simple prime-square increment rule clearly and accurately.

failures.A: Recurrence gives 19 rather than the fourth term 76.
failures.B: Essentially correct but underspecified about multiplier indexing and signs.
failures.C: Fits the continuation but describes only the tail, making it less general.
failures.D: No substantive failure; exact recurrence matches every term.
failures.E: Indexing/sign convention fails immediately at the second term.

simplicity.reason: D is the unique exact recurrence matching the supplied sequence from the stated initial value.

failures.A: Correct but less explicit about positional indexing.
failures.C: Correct but comparatively terse and ambiguous about which factorials begin the interleave.
failures.D: No substantive failure; positions and subsequences are explicit.

simplicity.reason: D gives the clearest exact interleaving of consecutive squares and factorials.
